PRETOLESI PRETOLESI
02The engineer

The ear behind the board.

Luca Pretolesi has spent twenty-five years and more than thirty thousand studio hours chasing one thing: the moment a record stops sounding mixed and starts sounding inevitable. Born in Genova. Built in Las Vegas. Trusted from São Paulo to Seoul.

DMI — Digital Music Innovation — pairs a hybrid analog/digital chain with a relentlessly modern ear. The result is a signature: loud without fatigue, wide without blur, low end you feel in your sternum.

03The signal chain — services

Four stages. One signal.

04Hear the master — live A/B

Hear the master.

Most people can’t name what mastering does. So don’t read it — move the fader. This runs a full mastering chain on the demo playing right now: the bus compressor leans in, the level lifts, the stereo field opens. Not a recording, not a reel — every change is computed live in your browser. A working model of the last two percent, made audible.

05Selected frequencies

You’ve heard us before.

Full discography on request — thousands of releases across dance, pop, latin & hip-hop.

06The room

True in here. True everywhere.

Behind two isolation doors, the control room holds a chain refined over a decade: an SPL analog mastering console, mastering-grade conversion, and a calibrated 7.1.4 immersive array — inside acoustics treated to within ±1.5 dB. What you approve here is what the world hears, on every system it owns.

Monitoring7.1.4 calibrated immersive array · reference SPL 85 dB · stereo mains + nearfield translation set
AnalogSPL mastering console & processing on 120-volt rails · custom hybrid summing · hand-matched chain
ConversionMastering-grade AD/DA · 96 kHz native sessions · clocked, measured, recalled per project
AcousticsBroadband trapping, QRD diffusion & soffit bass control · response flat within ±1.5 dB
LocationLas Vegas, Nevada · five minutes west of the Strip · in-room & fully remote attended sessions
07Studio I — step inside

Read the actual room.

Not a photo set — the real architecture. Studio I, drawn to its built spec: the 70° mix triangle, the soffit-mounted Focal mains, the relocated Vicoustic diffusion, the trapped soffit. Trace the plan, open a node — that’s the room you’ll book.

DMI’s immersive room. Studio D monitors in Dolby Atmos 7.1.4 — a bed of Focal mains and surrounds on the floor, a four-wide height array overhead, all under a floated diffusion cloud. Dolby- and Apple-certified. The measured plan is being drawn; the room is finished. Step through it.

The close-up stereo room. Studio M puts one chair on the nearfield axis between twin Focals, wrapped in cube-and-linear diffusion that holds a tight, honest picture — the room for detail work and decisions. The measured plan is being drawn.

BROOKLYN BROOKLYN
08The next room
BROOKLYN, NY — OPENING 2027
REV 0 · DRAFT FOR REVIEW · 40.6782° N / 73.9442° W

The next room.

NOT A SECOND LOCATION — A SECOND HOME FOR THE CRAFT

Fifteen years after Las Vegas, Studio DMI is building its second room: a members’ club in Brooklyn, layered over three working studios and a public bar. Built for the people who make the records — and the people who care that they were made well.

M-01Social

Membership in the room and at the bar.

Inquire →

M-02Studio

The rooms themselves. Booking rights members hold and the public doesn’t. Capped at one hundred fifty.

Inquire →

M-03Honorary

By invitation. For the artists whose work is the reason this room exists.

—

One bar. One menu. One price.

The bar belongs to the neighborhood. The rooms belong to the members. The drinks were never the point.

HOUSE DOCTRINE · POSTED AT THE BAR · 2027
Reference monitoring throughout · East-coast reference rooms · calibrated to the Las Vegas standard

DMI Brooklyn,
powered by FOCAL.
